items.title: NEW STORY: It's Neemias Queta's 5th time at Summer League.
items.author: Noa Dalzell
items.body_excerpt:
NEW STORY: It's Neemias Queta's 5th time at Summer League. 

The first 4 times, he was a player fighting to keep his NBA dreams alive.

This time, he showed up on the heels of a big-time extension. 

“I've been wishing for something like that to happen."
www.celticsblog.com/boston-celti...

media.title: Neemias Queta experienced a full-circle moment at Summer League
media.description: Queta returned to NBA Summer League for the fifth time this summer — but this time, as the manifestation of a dream come true.

"Jaylen’s a guy that you can’t replace in the city. Really, really hard to replace on the court as well. He did so much for Boston in general."

items.title: Neemias Queta last season: 10.2 PPG, 8.4 RPG, 1.3 BPG
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
Neemias Queta last season: 10.2 PPG, 8.4 RPG, 1.3 BPG

5th highest net rating in the NBA
5th in win shares per 48
3rd highest FG% in the NBA
3rd in EFG%
4th in TS%
8th in offensive rebounds
11th in rebounds
2nd in screen assists

He seized his opportunity and now gets contractually rewarded for it.

items.title: Awesome to see Neemias Queta land a long-term contract with a big raise. Very well-earned by Queta, who is continuing to improve year by year.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Awesome to see Neemias Queta land a long-term contract with a big raise. Very well-earned by Queta, who is continuing to improve year by year.

Celtics center spot is now covered for the next few seasons with Queta and Mitchell Robinson in the fold.

items.title: Boston picking up their team options for Neemias Queta and Jordan Walsh makes sense from managing the team salary under the tax and first apron for 2026-27.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Boston picking up their team options for Neemias Queta and Jordan Walsh makes sense from managing the team salary under the tax and first apron for 2026-27.

Both Queta and Walsh stay on a lower salary for this season and can be extended in-season to start new contracts in 2027-28.
